title: A Debate About Heathrow Airport's Big Plan
summary: Heathrow Airport in London wants to grow bigger by adding a new runway and other buildings. This plan costs £49 billion and might change roads like the M25. Jack Rankin, a local MP, says he is against this expansion because his area is already too crowded with traffic and noise from planes. He worries that more planes and cars will make life harder for people living nearby. However, he supports a new train link to the airport to help with transportation. The airport and government say expansion is urgent because Heathrow is full and needs to grow to help businesses and create jobs. Business groups agree it will bring benefits, but some people are concerned about the environment and local life.

keywords.explanation: A big airport in London where planes take off and land.
Making something bigger, like adding more runways or buildings.
A Member of Parliament, a person who represents people in government.
A long strip where planes land and take off at an airport.
Too many cars on the roads causing slow movement and jams.
A train connection to help people travel to and from a place.

options: His area is too crowded with traffic and noise
questions.correct_answer: His area is too crowded with traffic and noise

questions.correct_answer: Because Heathrow is full and needs to grow

questions.question: What might business groups think about the expansion?

background_read: Heathrow Airport is one of the busiest airports in the world, located near London. It helps people travel and businesses trade goods. When airports expand, they add more runways and facilities to handle more planes and passengers. This can create jobs but might also lead to more noise, traffic, and pollution for nearby communities. MPs like Jack Rankin represent local people in government and speak up about their concerns. Train links are important for reducing car traffic and making travel easier.
Article_Structure: WHO: Jack Rankin, an MP, and other groups like Heathrow Airport and the government.
WHAT: A debate about expanding Heathrow Airport with a new runway and buildings, while some support a train link.
WHERE: Heathrow Airport in London and the local area of Berkshire.
WHY: To address capacity issues and economic benefits versus concerns about traffic, noise, and pollution.

perspectives.description: Some people, like the airport and government, believe expansion is urgent to help businesses and create jobs because the airport is full.
Others, like MP Jack Rankin, are against it due to worries about increased traffic, noise, and pollution in the local area.
